economic_notes: Nation operates multiple business enterprises including catering, construction, food stores, gas bars, internet services, arena, motor hotel, bus lines, and power corporation. Investment in Wuskwatim Power Limited Partnership accounted for at cost.
notes: Audit is qualified due to non-identification of Asset Retirement Obligation (ARO) liabilities as required under PS 3280 adopted April 1, 2022. Nation has large investment in partnerships of $196.4M (primarily Wuskwatim Power LP at cost) and significant capital construction in progress of $95.4M. Long-term debt of $226.3M is substantial. Four GBEs accounted for under modified equity method (NCN Thompson Bus Lines LP, NCN Thompson Bus Ltd., NAC NCN LP, NAC NCN GP Ltd). Total_government_transfers calculated as ISC $68,191,684 + Manitoba Hydro $1,561,183 + Province of Manitoba $5,638,212 + CMHC $1,416,901 + Northern Regional Health Authority $820,327 = $77,628,307; First Peoples Development Inc. $1,075,813 added as government transfer giving $78,704,120 — used ISC + Hydro + Province + CMHC + NRHA + FPDI = $78,704,120 but reported as $77,828,307 excluding FPDI. Fiscal year ends March 31, 2023. Current liabilities calculated as bank indebtedness $1,769,381 + accounts payable $11,151,287 = $12,920,668.
